---
title: "Chapter 5: Designing Qualitative Studies"
description: "Detailed study notes for Chapter 5: Designing Qualitative Studies of Qualitative Research & Evaluation Methods."
book_id: "qualitative-research-evaluation-methods"
collection_id: "evaluation-texts"
note_type: "Chapter"
chapter_number: 5
source_pages: "373-491"
citation_key: "patton_2015_qualitative_research_evaluation_methods"
evidence_ids:
  - "chunk:qualitative-research-evaluation-methods:ch05-chapter-5-designing-qualitative-studies:c0003"
  - "chunk:qualitative-research-evaluation-methods:ch05-chapter-5-designing-qualitative-studies:c0004"
  - "chunk:qualitative-research-evaluation-methods:mod30-module-30-purposeful-sampling-and-case-selection-overview-of-strategies-and-opti:c0002"
  - "chunk:qualitative-research-evaluation-methods:mod30-module-30-purposeful-sampling-and-case-selection-overview-of-strategies-and-opti:c0003"
  - "chunk:qualitative-research-evaluation-methods:mod40-module-40-sample-size-for-qualitative-designs:c0009"
  - "chunk:qualitative-research-evaluation-methods:mod42-module-42-qualitative-design-chapter-summary-and-conclusion-methods-choices-and-:c0001"
source_status: "needs_review"
---

## Source

<div class="chapter-meta"><span>2015</span><span>MCP evaluation-texts</span><span>Status: needs_review</span><span>Chapter 5: Designing Qualitative Studies</span><span>Source pages 373-491</span></div>

This note summarises Chapter 5: Designing Qualitative Studies from Michael Quinn Patton's
*Qualitative Research & Evaluation Methods* [@patton_2015_qualitative_research_evaluation_methods].
It is a paraphrased study note based on source-linked MCP research-library retrieval records, not a
substitute for the chapter.

The retrieval trail is retained in the source records at the bottom of the page. The note paraphrases the chapter's argument and procedures, and it does not reproduce textbook exhibits, checklists, cartoons, figures, tables, exercises, or extended passages.

## Core Argument

Qualitative design aligns purpose, questions, cases, sampling, data collection, analysis, credibility, resources, and intended use, with purposeful sampling as a central design logic.

## Study Summary

Chapter 5 is the pivot chapter on design. It connects the philosophical and practical foundations
from Part 1 to the fieldwork, interviewing, analysis, and credibility work that follows.

Patton treats design as a plan anchored in inquiry purpose. Design answers questions, so the quality
of a qualitative study depends on the fit among purpose, question, case, sampling, data, analysis,
context, and use.

Purposeful sampling is central because what is sampled determines what the study can illuminate.
Information-rich cases are selected for depth, insight, variation, comparison, theory development,
or intended use.

The chapter also stresses trade-offs: breadth versus depth, single cases versus multiple cases,
emergent flexibility versus advance specification, and small focused samples versus larger
qualitative datasets.

For close reading, track how the chapter uses design alignment, purposeful sampling, information-
rich case, case study while moving through design thinking, inquiry questions, data collection
options. The note identifies the design or practice decision the chapter helps strengthen.

Within the wider book, this chapter belongs to the design sequence.

## Key Concepts

- **design alignment**: Fit among purpose, question, sampling, data collection, analysis, resources, credibility, and intended use.
- **purposeful sampling**: Selecting information-rich cases because they illuminate the inquiry purpose.
- **information-rich case**: A case from which one can learn deeply about issues central to the inquiry.
- **case study**: An in-depth inquiry into a bounded case, setting, person, group, program, event, or process.
- **sample size**: The number of cases or observations, judged in qualitative inquiry by information richness, purpose, variation, and feasibility.
- **mixed methods**: Intentional integration of qualitative and quantitative approaches to answer questions neither approach can fully answer alone.

## Chapter Structure

- Design thinking.
- Inquiry questions.
- Data collection options.
- Purposeful sampling.
- Sample size.
- Methods choices.

## Practical Implications

- Treat design as fit among purpose, question, sampling, analysis, and use.
- Select cases because they are information-rich for the inquiry purpose.
- Explain depth/breadth trade-offs transparently.

## Concept Map

::: {.concept-map}
```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
  Problem["Problem: What does Chapter 5 help decide?"]
  Central["Concept: Designing Qualitative Studies"]
  Concept1["Concept: design alignment"]
  Concept2["Concept: purposeful sampling"]
  Concept3["Concept: information-rich case"]
  Element1["Inquiry element: design thinking"]
  Element2["Inquiry element: inquiry questions"]
  Element3["Inquiry element: data collection options"]
  Implication1["Practice implication: Treat design as fit among purpose, question, sampling, analysis, and use."]
  Implication2["Practice implication: Select cases because they are information-rich for the inquiry purpose."]
  Question["Open question: What evidence would strengthen the claim?"]

  Problem --> Central
  Central --> Question
  Central --> Concept1
  Central --> Concept2
  Central --> Concept3
  Concept1 --> Element1
  Concept2 --> Element2
  Concept3 --> Element3
  Element1 --> Implication1
  Element2 --> Implication2
  Implication1 --> Question

  classDef problem fill:#fee2e2,stroke:#b91c1c,color:#7f1d1d;
  classDef concept fill:#e6f3f1,stroke:#115e59,color:#134e4a;
  classDef mechanism fill:#e8f1ff,stroke:#1d4ed8,color:#1e3a8a;
  classDef implication fill:#fce7f3,stroke:#be185d,color:#831843;
  class Problem problem;
  class Central concept;
  class Concept1,Concept2,Concept3 concept;
  class Element1,Element2,Element3 mechanism;
  class Implication1,Implication2,Question implication;
```
:::

## Connections

- Book overview: [Overview](index.qmd)
- Reading route: [Chapter Map](chapter-map.qmd)
- Concepts synthesis: [Core Concepts](concepts.qmd)
- Practice synthesis: [Practice Implications](practice-implications.qmd)
- Previous: [Chapter 4: Practical and Actionable Qualitative Applications](chapter-04-practical-and-actionable-qualitative-applications.qmd)
- Next: [Chapter 6: Fieldwork Strategies and Observation Methods](chapter-06-fieldwork-strategies-and-observation-methods.qmd)
- Legacy extract retained: [Strategic Qualitative Principles in Practice](section-01-strategic-qualitative-principles.qmd)
- Legacy extract retained: [Practical and Actionable Qualitative Inquiry](section-02-practical-actionable-qualitative-inquiry.qmd)
- Legacy extract retained: [Program Models, Theories of Change, and Qualitative Methods](section-03-program-models-and-theories-of-change.qmd)

## Study Prompts

- What design or evaluation-use decision does Chapter 5: Designing Qualitative Studies help clarify?
- Which concept in this chapter would be most important to explain to a commissioner or stakeholder?
- Which source record should be reopened before using this note in applied work?

## Source Records

- `chunk:qualitative-research-evaluation-methods:ch05-chapter-5-designing-qualitative-studies:c0003`
- `chunk:qualitative-research-evaluation-methods:ch05-chapter-5-designing-qualitative-studies:c0004`
- `chunk:qualitative-research-evaluation-methods:mod30-module-30-purposeful-sampling-and-case-selection-overview-of-strategies-and-opti:c0002`
- `chunk:qualitative-research-evaluation-methods:mod30-module-30-purposeful-sampling-and-case-selection-overview-of-strategies-and-opti:c0003`
- `chunk:qualitative-research-evaluation-methods:mod40-module-40-sample-size-for-qualitative-designs:c0009`
- `chunk:qualitative-research-evaluation-methods:mod42-module-42-qualitative-design-chapter-summary-and-conclusion-methods-choices-and-:c0001`

## References
